sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Oklo Expands Nuclear Waste And Isotope Platform While Shares Trail Targets
feed.summary: Oklo (NYSE:OKLO) is expanding its nuclear energy platform by making progress in nuclear waste management and acquiring Atomic Alchemy to add medical and industrial isotope production capabilities. These advancements broaden Oklo's business beyond compact advanced reactors, diversifying its potential revenue streams into energy and healthcare. Despite these developments, Oklo currently has no revenue, is loss-making, and its shares are trading significantly below analyst targets, indicating execution risk and potential for further dilution.

feed.title: Ollie’s Bargain Outlet shares drop 6.6% after mixed Q3 report
feed.summary: Ollie's Bargain Outlet (OLLI) shares experienced a 6.6% decline following a mixed third-quarter earnings report. The discount retailer reported adjusted earnings per share that beat analyst expectations, but its revenue fell short. The market's reaction suggests concerns despite the earnings beat.

feed.title: Oil-Dri Q1 earnings decline Y/Y amid tough comparison pressures
feed.summary: This article mentions that Oil-Dri's Q1 earnings declined year-over-year. This decrease is attributed to tough comparison pressures from the previous year.

feed.title: Garmin Ltd. schedules first quarter 2026 earnings call – Company Announcement
feed.summary: Garmin Ltd. (NYSE: GRMN) has scheduled its first quarter 2026 earnings conference call for Wednesday, April 29, 2026, at 10:30 a.m. EDT. Shareholders and investors can register online to access the call, which will coincide with the release of the company's earnings report. A live webcast will be available on Garmin's investor relations website, with an archive accessible for one year.

feed.title: Evercore ISI Adjusts PT on FactSet Research Systems to $265 From $321, Maintains In Line Rating
feed.summary: Evercore ISI has adjusted its price target for FactSet Research Systems (NYSE: FDS) to $265, down from $321. The firm has decided to maintain an "In Line" rating for the company. This update reflects Evercore ISI's latest outlook on FactSet's valuation and market position.

feed.title: Ovintiv (NYSE:OVV) Raised to Strong-Buy at Truist Financial
feed.summary: Truist Financial upgraded Ovintiv (NYSE:OVV) to a "strong-buy" rating. Ovintiv's stock opened at $59.00 and has a market capitalization of $16.72 billion. The company recently reported strong earnings, beating analyst estimates, and has seen insider selling from executive Meghan Nicole Eilers and director Howard John Mayson.

feed.title: News | Recognizing the deals and projects that made a difference
feed.summary: The 2026 CoStar Impact Awards honor exemplary commercial real estate transactions and projects from 2025 that significantly impacted major U.S. and Canadian markets. Industry professionals selected the winners, recognizing their leadership and the lasting value they bring to their communities. Lisa Ruggles, senior vice president of global operations at CoStar Group, commended the strength of this year's submissions and the judges' diligent selections.

feed.title: Kempner Capital Management Inc. Sells 19,374 Shares of Bank of America Corporation $BAC
feed.summary: Kempner Capital Management Inc. reduced its stake in Bank of America Corporation (NYSE:BAC) by 30.1% during the fourth quarter, selling 19,374 shares. The firm now holds 44,962 shares of Bank of America, valued at $2,488,000, making it their 21st largest position. Several other institutional investors also adjusted their holdings in Bank of America, while analysts have given the stock a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average price target of $59.87.

feed.title: Amex launches Graphite Business Cash Card with 2% cash back
feed.summary: American Express has introduced the new Graphite Business Cash Unlimited Card, offering 2% cash back on all eligible purchases and 5% cash back on flights and prepaid hotels booked through Amex Travel, with a $295 annual fee. While it offers a simple earning structure, the article suggests that its limited flexibility, high spending thresholds for unlocking benefits, and the existence of more compelling alternatives from competitors and within Amex's own lineup might make it attractive only to a narrow group of businesses already embedded in the Amex ecosystem.

feed.title: Honeywell bets $500M to speed navigation and missile system output
feed.summary: Honeywell Aerospace has signed a supplier framework agreement with the U.S. Department of War, committing $500 million over multiple years to boost the production capacity of critical defense technologies. This investment will accelerate the output of navigation systems, Assure actuators, and electronic warfare solutions to support U.S. and allied forces, positioning Honeywell as a key Tier 1 supplier. The move comes as part of a broader push to secure long-term defense demand and follows recent capital structure actions by Honeywell related to the planned Aerospace spin-off.

feed.title: AI data centers wait years for power. This project says 6-12 months
feed.summary: GridCARE and National Grid have announced a partnership to accelerate connecting large-load customers, particularly AI data centers, to the power grid. They aim to reduce the time-to-energization from several years to 6-12 months by using an AI-driven platform that identifies and unlocks underutilized grid capacity. This collaboration is expected to manage the surging demand for power from AI infrastructure while maintaining grid reliability and affordability for customers in New York.

feed.title: Insider Sell: Michael Rosenbaum Sells Shares of Guidewire Softwa
feed.summary: Michael Rosenbaum, CEO of Guidewire Software Inc, sold 1,200 shares on March 23, 2026, reducing his holdings to 220,006 shares. This transaction is part of a larger trend where he has sold 116,194 shares over the past year with no purchases. Guidewire Software, trading at $155.56, appears modestly undervalued with a price-to-GF-Value ratio of 0.81, despite a high P/E ratio compared to the industry median.

feed.title: American Express Unveils New Graphite™ Business Cash Unlimited Card, Rewarding Business Owners with Unlimited 2% Cash Back
feed.summary: American Express has introduced the new Graphite™ Business Cash Unlimited Card, offering business owners unlimited 2% cash back on all eligible purchases and unlimited 5% cash back on flights and prepaid hotel bookings through American Express Travel. The metal card, which features a carbon fiber-inspired design, aims to help businesses manage cash flow, fuel growth, and strengthen their bottom line with features like No Preset Spending Limit and Pay Over Time options. The card, which comes with an annual fee of $295, also includes integrated business tools like Virtual Cards, Employee Cards, and a potential $2,400 One AP statement credit, all backed by American Express's world-class service.

feed.title: Four robots installed 100 MW of solar at one California site
feed.summary: Maximo, a solar robotics company incubated by The AES Corporation, has announced the successful installation of 100 MW of utility-scale solar capacity at AES' Bellefield complex using a four-robot fleet. This achievement demonstrates that intelligent field robotics can deliver sustained commercial production, exceeding one module per minute and significantly boosting regional productivity. The project highlights the role of robotics in addressing increasing demand for electricity and labor constraints within the solar construction industry, with support from NVIDIA and AWS for development and AI-driven operations.

feed.title: Los Angeles fuel pipeline proposal gets more time to sign up shippers
feed.summary: Phillips 66 and Kinder Morgan have extended the second open season for their proposed Western Gateway Pipeline, a refined products pipeline system, until April 15. This extension provides potential shippers with more time to evaluate revised terms and secure internal approvals for capacity. The pipeline aims to deliver refined products into the Los Angeles market via an existing Kinder Morgan line.

feed.title: Merck to acquire Terns Pharmaceuticals for $53 per share in cash
feed.summary: This article announces that Merck plans to acquire Terns Pharmaceuticals. The acquisition will be an all-cash deal, with Merck paying $53 per share for Terns Pharmaceuticals.

feed.title: Merck to buy Terns for $6.7 billion
feed.summary: The article states that Merck is set to acquire Terns for $6.7 billion. No further details are provided in the current content.

feed.title: FedEx reports $120M headwind
feed.summary: FedEx CFO John Dietrich announced that the MD-11 grounding cost the company $120 million in the most recent quarter. This figure is attributed to increased operating costs and a loss of revenue for the transportation giant.

feed.title: Insider Selling: Monolithic Power Systems (NASDAQ:MPWR) EVP Sells 30 Shares of Stock

This follows larger sales in February. Despite insider selling, the company's Q4 earnings beat expectations, revenue increased 20.8% year-over-year, and the quarterly dividend was raised to $2.00.

feed.title: 5 Most Promising Small-Cap Industrial Stocks Under $30
feed.summary: This article identifies five promising small-cap industrial stocks trading under $30, with a focus on NANO Nuclear Energy Inc. (NASDAQ: NNE) as the first featured company. NANO Nuclear Energy is highlighted for its advancements in a proprietary transportation solution for high-assay low-enriched uranium fuel and its strategy to develop a vertically integrated nuclear platform. The company, currently in a pre-revenue stage, aims for commercialization around the early 2030s with its various microreactor designs.

feed.title: Amcor to launch recyclable airless dispenser for beauty industry
feed.summary: Amcor is set to launch "Magic One," a new recyclable airless dispensing system for the beauty and personal care industry. This dispenser, made entirely of polyolefin, offers multiple output volumes and is designed for recyclability and compliance with EU regulations. Production will be in Germany, with deliveries beginning in early 2027.

feed.title: TD Cowen Adjusts Price Target on Conagra Brands to $14 From $18, Maintains Hold Rating
feed.summary: TD Cowen has lowered its price target for Conagra Brands (CAG) from $18 to $14, while keeping a "Hold" rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a revised outlook from the analyst firm. The article also notes recent similar price target adjustments and rating changes for Conagra by other financial institutions.

feed.title: Merck to Acquire Terns Pharmaceuticals, Inc., Expanding Its Hematology Pipeline With TERN-701, a Novel Candidate for Chronic Myeloid Leukemia (CML)
feed.summary: Merck announced its acquisition of Terns Pharmaceuticals, Inc. for approximately $6.7 billion, aiming to expand its hematology pipeline. The acquisition centers on TERN-701, a novel oral allosteric BCR::ABL1 tyrosine kinase inhibitor for chronic myeloid leukemia (CML), currently in Phase 1/2 development. This move is expected to diversify Merck's oncology portfolio and provide a potentially differentiated treatment option for CML patients.

feed.title: Bio-Techne expands spatial biology product portfolio
feed.summary: Bio-Techne Corporation has expanded its COMET spatial biology solution portfolio with the introduction of SPYRE Focus Panels and SPYRE Amplification Kits, alongside new features for its HORIZON Image Analysis Software. These additions aim to provide researchers with a more flexible, faster, and higher sensitivity workflow for achieving deeper insights in spatial biology. The company, which maintains a strong financial position with 19 consecutive years of dividend payments, also recently surpassed its Q2 fiscal year 2026 earnings and revenue forecasts, leading to increased price targets from analysts like Stifel, Argus, and TD Cowen.

feed.title: Medtronic (MDT) Announces FDA Approval for Expanded Indication of OmniaSecure
feed.summary: Medtronic (NYSE:MDT) announced FDA approval for an expanded indication of its OmniaSecure defibrillation lead, allowing placement in the left bundle branch area for conduction system pacing to better mimic natural heart physiology. The company also recently agreed to acquire Scientia Vascular for $550M. Additionally, Medtronic reported strong Q3 earnings, surpassing revenue and EPS expectations.

feed.title: Argus Lowers its Price Target on Becton, Dickinson and Company (BDX) to $180 from $230
feed.summary: Argus has lowered its price target for Becton, Dickinson and Company (NYSE:BDX) to $180 from $230 but maintained a Buy rating. This decision is based on the company's transition to a pure-play medtech model and its stock trading at a lower forward earnings multiple compared to its peers. Becton, Dickinson also recently received FDA clearance for a new surgical irrigation system and reported strong Q1 results, exceeding analyst estimates for both EPS and revenue.

feed.title: Argus reiterates Moody’s stock buy rating on AI opportunity
feed.summary: Argus has reconfirmed its Buy rating and $550 price target for Moody's Corp (NYSE:MCO) stock, viewing concerns about AI as a buying opportunity. The firm believes Moody's proprietary data makes it resilient to AI displacement, unlike other financial information service companies. This reiteration follows Moody's strong Q4 2025 earnings which exceeded analyst expectations and a 10% dividend increase in February 2025.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley downgrades Shell stock rating on limited upside versus peers
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley downgraded Shell PLC (NYSE:SHEL) to Equalweight from Overweight, setting a new price target of GBP35.89. The firm noted that while Shell's defensive attributes like diversified earnings and strong LNG positioning remain intact, they offer less differentiation and growth upside compared to peers in a rising price environment. Shell’s per-share free cash flow growth is now seen as more reliant on cost savings and share count reduction.

feed.title: i-80 Gold Secures $250 Million Financing, Fully Funds Nevada Development
feed.summary: i-80 Gold Corp. has secured a $250 million financing package, including a $150 million gold prepayment facility and a $100 million accordion feature, which fully funds its Nevada development plans. This recapitalization brings the company's total capital raised to over $1 billion, enabling it to advance five gold projects and refurbish the Lone Tree processing plant. The financing is expected to significantly increase i-80 Gold's annual gold production to 300,000-400,000 ounces by 2031, positioning it as a mid-tier gold producer.

feed.title: U.S. Compound Management Market Is Going to Boom | Thermo Fisher
feed.summary: Coherent Market Insights has published a report on the "U.S. Compound Management Market: Industry Trends, Share, Size, Growth, Opportunity, and Forecast 2026-2033". The report provides an in-depth analysis of market performance, competition, regional distribution, and growth potential, making it a valuable tool for entrepreneurs, investors, and business strategists. It also includes key market players, segmentation by end-user, research methodology, and strategic points of analysis.

feed.title: Apollo Global (APO) caps redemptions at 5% for Apollo Debt Solutions

This move follows the company's Q4 earnings call where executives highlighted strong fundraising across various credit strategies, positioning them well for an expected increase in retail demand. The redemption cap aims to manage liquidity within the fund.

feed.title: Antitrust Lawsuit Alleges Vail Resorts and Alterra Inflated Lift Ticket Prices to Force Season Pass Sales
feed.summary: A federal class-action antitrust lawsuit has been filed against Vail Resorts and Alterra Mountain Company, alleging they deliberately inflated single-day lift ticket prices to coerce skiers and snowboarders into purchasing season passes. The lawsuit claims this practice violates antitrust laws by restricting competition and consumer choice, as day tickets now routinely exceed $300 while season passes appear to offer better value. The plaintiffs are seeking treble damages, injunctive relief, and disgorgement of profits, subjecting the modern multi-resort pass system to its most significant legal scrutiny to date.
